food_subgroup: Non-food decorative item — knitted/crochet craft gift; not a consumable food product

ontology.foodon_gap_note: This item is not a food. It is a handmade knitted decorative rose bouquet. No FoodOn URI applies. FoodOn ontology covers consumable food items only.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 covers food and feed commodities for human and animal consumption. This item is a non-consumable decorative craft product and has no applicable FoodEx2 code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT clinical terminology does not cover decorative non-food craft items. No applicable code exists.

serving.description: 1 bouquet (7 knitted roses) — not a food serving
serving.natural_language_portion: This item is not consumed as food. One bouquet consists of 7 handmade knitted pink roses and is sold as a decorative gift item, not a meal or edible portion.

The item explicitly states it is NOT fresh roses and is a handmade craft gift. No nutritional data exists or is applicable. This record is flagged as a non-food item that should not be in a food database.

GL per serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. gi_category is low by convention. No GI data exists or is applicable for a knitted decorative bouquet.

hypertension.key_factor: Non-food item — no sodium, no nutrients, no dietary impact
hypertension.guidance: This item is a decorative knitted rose bouquet and is not consumed as food; it has no sodium content and poses no dietary concern for individuals managing hypertension.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Non-food item — zero carbohydrate, zero glycemic load
type2_diabetes.guidance: This item is a decorative knitted rose bouquet and is not consumed as food; it contains no carbohydrates and has no glycemic impact, making it irrelevant to blood glucose management for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Non-food item — no fat, no cholesterol, no dietary impact
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This item is a decorative knitted rose bouquet and is not consumed as food; it contains no fat, saturated fat, or cholesterol and poses no cardiovascular dietary risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Heartstrings Soft Embrace (7 Rose Bouquet) is a handmade decorative gift item, not a food product. It consists of seven fully knitted or crocheted pink roses crafted from yarn or textile fibre, designed to be a long-lasting alternative to fresh cut flowers. The item is sold on field survey data under The Noodle Place - Circular Road as a gift product. The product description explicitly states it is NOT fresh roses. It is described as plush, warm, and comforting — a sentimental gift intended to evoke the feeling of a floral bouquet without wilting. This item has no nutritional value, no caloric content, no macronutrients, and no micronutrients. It should not be classified as a food item in any food database. Its presence in a food delivery platform listing is an artefact of the platform allowing non-food gift items to be sold alongside food orders. From a clinical and nutritional standpoint, this item is entirely irrelevant — it cannot be consumed, digested, or metabolised. It poses no dietary risk or benefit for any health condition including hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. This record is flagged for review and potential removal from the food database, as it does not represent a consumable food product.
common_user_queries: What is the Heartstrings Soft Embrace rose bouquet?
Is the 7 rose bouquet from field survey edible?
What are the calories in the knitted rose bouquet?

Can I eat the roses from The Noodle Place?
What is a knitted rose bouquet gift?

confidence_reason: This item is not a food product. No nutritional data exists or is applicable. All nutrient values are set to confirmed zero as this is a non-consumable decorative craft item. Confidence is low because the record itself is anomalous — it represents a non-food item erroneously listed in a food delivery context. No approved nutritional source covers this item.
notes: CRITICAL FLAG: This record (GD002819) represents a non-food decorative item — a handmade knitted rose bouquet — that was listed on field survey data under The Noodle Place - Circular Road. The item explicitly states it is NOT fresh roses and is a craft gift product. This record should be reviewed for removal from the food database as it does not represent any consumable food product. All core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are confirmed zero — this is a non-food item that contains no nutrients by definition; confirmed zero per item description and nature of product. All secondary nutrients are also confirmed zero for the same reason. All micronutrient fields are set to 0.0 (confirmed zero — non-food item). glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this item contains no glycemic carbohydrates and is not consumed; gi_category low per convention for non-food zero-carbohydrate items. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate_g is 0. Multilingual names are provided in English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean cuisine rules, as the item is listed under a Singapore food delivery platform. Ontology codes are all null with match_type none — no food ontology system covers non-food decorative craft items. This record is a FAIL because it cannot achieve a meaningful PASS as a food record — it is not food. The record is preserved for audit purposes with all fields populated to the extent possible given the non-food nature of the item.

q1_food_identity.notes: Food name accurately identifies this as a non-food decorative item. The canonical name is honest and precise about the nature of the item.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros are present as numeric values (0.0). Zero values are justified: confirmed zero — non-food item contains no nutrients by definition. Nutrient source clearly documents the non-food nature.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: All secondary nutrient fields (dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, saturated_fat_g, sugar_g) are present as numeric values (0.0). Confirmed zero for non-food item.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to 1 (sentinel for non-food/zero-carb items per convention), gi_category low, glycemic_load_per_serving 0.0, gi_source documented. Convention applied per Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule adapted for non-food items.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: All ontology codes are null with match_type none. This is honest — no food ontology system covers non-food decorative craft items. However, this means no defensible ontology anchor exists, which is a Q5 failure per scoring rules requiring at least one defensible anchor. Gap notes are fully documented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: Natural language description exceeds 200 characters, explains what the item is, its non-food nature, cultural context as a gift item, and clinical irrelevance. Suitable for RAG/LLM use to correctly identify and flag this as a non-food item.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease. Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence appropriate to a non-food item.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: Aliases and aliases_by_language are consistent and comprehensive for a non-food item. However, this item has no natural food aliases, regional food variants, or culinary synonyms. Coverage is limited by the non-food nature of the item. The flat aliases list contains all strings from aliases_by_language. Q8 fails because meaningful multilingual food alias coverage cannot be achieved for a non-food craft item — the aliases provided are descriptive labels rather than genuine food synonyms.

failure_reasons: Q5 FAIL: No defensible ontology anchor exists — this item is not a food and no food ontology system (FoodOn, FoodEx2, SNOMED CT) covers non-food decorative craft items. All ontology codes are null.

Q8 FAIL: Meaningful multilingual food alias coverage cannot be achieved for a non-food decorative craft item. Aliases provided are descriptive labels rather than genuine culinary synonyms or regional food name variants.

FUNDAMENTAL ISSUE: GD002819 is not a food product. It is a handmade knitted rose bouquet sold as a gift item on field survey data. This record should be reviewed for removal from the food database. A food record cannot achieve a full PASS for an item that is not food.
improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft produced but resulted in invalid JSON (gemini engine failure).
Iteration 2: Second draft produced but resulted in invalid JSON (gemini engine failure).

Iteration 3: Complete rebuild of JSON from scratch with strict validity checking. Identified fundamental issue: GD002819 is a non-food decorative item. All fields populated to maximum extent possible. Zero values justified with confirmed zero notation. GI set to 1 per convention. Multilingual names added per Singapore rules. Ontology set to none with full gap documentation. Health context populated with appropriate non-food clinical notes. LLM description written to flag non-food nature for RAG systems.
Confirmed all aliases_by_language entries appear verbatim in flat aliases list.
Confirmed all -1 sentinel values replaced with 0.0 (confirmed zero) for non-food item.
Confirmed GI set to 1 with gi_category low per zero-carbohydrate convention.
Score achieved: 5/8. Q5 and Q8 cannot pass for a non-food item. FAIL is the correct and honest outcome.

The item is explicitly described as NOT fresh roses and is a handmade craft gift. All hard gates pass. Score is 5/8: Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4, Q6, Q7 pass; Q5 fails (no ontology anchor possible for non-food item); Q8 fails (no genuine food aliases possible).
